original_abstract: Educational evaluation is a vital mechanism for identifying the specific strengths and weaknesses of academic initiatives, providing the empirical basis required for informed decision-making and sustainable development. This study analyzes the core concepts, objectives, principles, and procedural stages of educational program evaluation, while also identifying the catalysts and barriers to its successful implementation. Employing a literature review methodology, the research synthesizes academic theories and existing studies to establish a framework for assessing program effectiveness and enhancing educational quality. The findings indicate that effective evaluation must be grounded in objectivity and stay strictly aligned with predetermined program goals. The essential phases of the process include systematic preparation, rigorous execution, and consistent monitoring. Results further highlight that clear governmental and institutional policies serve as primary supporting factors, whereas progress is frequently impeded by financial constraints and a lack of specialized expertise. To address these challenges, the study suggests fostering a positive organizational culture regarding evaluation and increasing stakeholder engagement—including parents and the community—to ensure that assessment outcomes drive meaningful improvements in the national education system.
humanized_abstract: ABSTRACT The purpose of educational evaluation is to discover the relative strengths and weaknesses of educational programs and to form a basis for informed planning and sustainable development. This study has aimed at the identification of the concepts, purposes, principles and phases of educational program evaluation, the facilitators and obstacles of the evaluation process of the educational programs and making recommendations accordingly. According to the study method, literature review was used in the study and the relevant studies and theories were brought together and summarized in a way to evaluate the educational program effectiveness and to improve the educational quality. Results showed that, educational evaluations should be based on objectivity and carried out in line with the goals set for educational programs. It is also important to have phases such as planning, implementation and controlling in a systematic manner for educational evaluation. In addition, the supportive governmental and institutional regulations constitute the major facilitating factors, whereas obstacles are mostly financial and expert-based. For this reason, it is necessary to create an evaluation-friendly environment in institutions and to increase the participation of stakeholders including parents and society towards transforming the assessment findings into substantial improvements in the national education system. Keywords: Evaluation, Educational Evaluation, Facilitators and Obstacles of Evaluation Process.
